shortAnswer: Progesterone is produced after ovulation and prepares the body for a possible pregnancy. Low values in the second half of the cycle can be related to cycle disorders or an absent ovulation, and are often measured as part of a fertility workup.
metaDescription: Progesterone too low? Meaning for cycle and fertility explained simply.
doctorQuestions: What does my progesterone value mean for my cycle or fertility?
Should the value be measured again at a different time?
